mechanism_narrative: A rising level of nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) reflects expanding aggregate income and consumption demand, which translates into higher expected corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2011 payrolls at 131028 thousand showing recovery trend
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not positive in more than 55% of months when PAYEMS is increasing, or rank correlation between PAYEMS change and 20-day returns is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned at or below 0.25 percent, the zero lower bound constraint forces the Federal Reserve to rely on unconventional monetary tools and forward guidance, which suppress long-term real yields and push investors out the risk curve into equities, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Effective federal funds rate at 0.1% as of April 2011 during QE2
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative or insignificantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.10) during all periods when FEDFUNDS <= 0.25% after 2008.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in industrial production and retail sales captures a broad-based demand recovery where both manufacturing output and household spending are expanding together; this joint movement validates revenue growth sustainability and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO and RSAFS changes lacks predictive power, with rank correlation below 0.03, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of joint acceleration episodes.

mechanism_narrative: During a zero lower bound regime, a rising consumer price index signals successful reflation that erodes the real burden of debt and supports nominal corporate pricing power, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days; this mechanism inverts when the federal funds rate is above 1.5 percent.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%, rising CPIAUCSL fails to coincide with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations, or the spread between inflation-driven signal returns and baseline returns is not statistically positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Monthly growth in the industrial production index directly measures the physical volume of output from the manufacturing, mining, and utility sectors; rising output signals capacity utilization recovery and operating leverage gains for corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day S&P 500 returns occur in fewer than 52% of weeks when INDPRO is increasing month-over-month, or rank IC is below 0.02 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls indicates that residential investment recovery is supported by income growth rather than speculative leverage; this joint signal of sustainable construction demand and labor market health drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising HOUST and rising PAYEMS fails to generate positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of joint occurrences, or the interaction term rank correlation falls below 0.03 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate persistently above 8.5 percent signals deficient aggregate demand and weak household income formation that compresses consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 8.5%, or the rank correlation between UNRATE level and 5-day returns is positive and statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured by GDPC1 captures the aggregate value of final goods and services produced in the economy; positive quarterly momentum in real output confirms macro recovery and lifts the earnings denominator in equity valuation models, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarter-over-quarter growth in GDPC1 shows rank correlation below 0.03 with subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns over a 3-year rolling window, or positive GDP growth quarters fail to deliver positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of cases.
